notes: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Transfer students are encouraged to complete as many major preparation courses as possible prior to transfer. Please note the courses that are required for admissions in the below agreement. Please refer to the Transfer Admissions Requirements for additional information on admissions to this major.

Guarantee your admission to UC Davis! The UC Davis Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a written agreement ensuring your admission to UC Davis from any California Community College. Please visit the TAG webpage for more information!

Transfer students are strongly encouraged to work closely with the California Community College counselors for transfer preparation to UC Davis. Transfer students may also contact a UC Davis transfer advisor for assistance. The below agreements should be utilized in conjunction with professional advising.
Please note that the Department Agreements as published on ASSIST are also important documents and should be referenced in conjunction with the major agreements as needed.
Group 2, Group 3 and Group 4: Other course combinations may be acceptable. Please refer to the major and an advisor.

The Human Biology major provides fundamental knowledge needed for a broad range of careers, including those in the areas of healthcare, biotechnology, public health, public policy, and education, and for advanced study in health-related disciplines, including medicine, dentistry, nursing, physical therapy, and pharmacy.

The Human Biology major provides students with a broad biological understanding of our species, from molecules, genes, and cells to tissues, organ systems and organism/environment interactions. The curriculum includes classes on the basic principles that help us understand normal human physiology, human health, and the molecular basis of disease. In the freshman and sophomore years, students majoring in Human Biology build a broad scientific background, taking courses in chemistry, biology, physics, and mathematics. As juniors or seniors, students can enroll in a variety of courses focused on biological processes and diseases that affect humans.

The Human Biology major is currently only open to students through a lottery. Incoming College of Biological Sciences (CBS) transfer students occurs each Fall for as long as this restriction is in place. All incoming CBS transfer students are eligible to enter the major. For more information about the lottery, see: https://biology.ucdavis.edu/undergraduate/majors-minors
